active_themes.label: Warsh Confirmation Spotlight
active_themes.summary: Warsh testified before Congress on April 21. He maintained stated commitment to Fed independence, fielded crypto-holdings questions without conceding conflict, and has not faced hard blocking votes. The confirmation path remains open but not accelerated. What remains unresolved: whether his eventual confirmation—or Powell's forced exit—will be perceived by markets as a normal succession or as political capture of the institution during an active geopolitical crisis. If confirmation happens while Iran tensions spike and oil rises, markets may reprice the entire Fed credibility regime.

active_themes.label: Iran War: Bet on Talks Holding
active_themes.summary: US announced ceasefire extension today. Oil traders continue pricing assumption that talks restart in meaningful form before supply destruction becomes irreversible. Real economic damage is visible (Spirit Airlines liquidation, EasyJet guidance cut, UK CO2 contingency planning) but remains contained. The open question is still whether actual supply losses force the market to reprice the blockade duration from 3 weeks to 3-6 months. Ceasefire extension bought time but didn't resolve the underlying tension.

active_themes.label: Norm-Breaking as Operating Model
active_themes.summary: Pattern deepens today: Warsh confirmation hearings occur while Powell is simultaneously under investigation (likely Trump-linked pressure); DOJ investigating SPLC; NASA investigating scientist deaths with tech ties; Sullivan & Cromwell apologizing for AI hallucinations in legal filings. This is not isolated scandal—it is a system where institutional decisions are increasingly made under informal pressure or threat of political/legal consequence. Markets have not yet priced the cost of decisions being made for political rather than economic reasons during a dual crisis (Fed succession + geopolitical risk).

open_watchlist.condition: Iran announces official delegation for Pakistan talks within 48 hours
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms the blockade/negotiation gambit is real and not theater; de-risks a war scenario and supports continued oil decline and bank rally.

open_watchlist.condition: 10-year Treasury yield drops below 3.8% or rises above 4.2%
open_watchlist.meaning: A drop would confirm Fed-dovish pricing is sticky; a rise would signal bond markets are pricing inflation risk from the policy regime change.

open_watchlist.condition: Oil (WTI) closes above $95/barrel for two consecutive days
open_watchlist.meaning: Supply destruction is real and market reprices Fed's reaction function; equities likely down 2-4% as inflation expectations rise and rate-cut bets shrink.

open_watchlist.condition: Iran announces official negotiating delegation for Pakistan talks (within 48-72 hours)
open_watchlist.meaning: Blockade is functioning as a negotiating tool, not a permanent rupture; risk premium should compress and oil should retreat 5-8%.

open_watchlist.condition: Powell makes any public statement acknowledging Trump's May timeline or hinting at stepping down
open_watchlist.meaning: Fed independence has cracked publicly; market reprices regime change risk and Warsh's confirmation odds spike sharply upward.

open_watchlist.condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises above 4.2% while S&P stays flat or down
open_watchlist.meaning: Fed expectations are decoupling from equity valuations—bond market is pricing stagflation faster than stocks; sell signal for duration and growth.

open_watchlist.condition: Iran announces official negotiating delegation within 48 hours
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms oil traders' bet that talks restart before supply destruction becomes irreversible; keeps oil below $95 and reduces tail risk of 3-6 month blockade.

open_watchlist.condition: WTI oil closes above $95 for two consecutive trading sessions
open_watchlist.meaning: Signals market repricing of Iran supply crisis duration or severity; forces simultaneous reassessment of Fed independence risk if Warsh confirms at same time oil is spiking.

open_watchlist.condition: Fed official (Powell, governor, or staff) makes public statement on rates that sounds politically motivated rather than data-driven
open_watchlist.meaning: First explicit signal that institutional capture has moved from theory to practice; would likely trigger equities sell-off and 10-year yield spike.

open_watchlist.condition: Senate Democrats signal willingness to vote against Warsh confirmation (not delay, but actual opposition votes above 10 senators)
open_watchlist.meaning: Would slow timeline and force Warsh to make explicit commitments on Fed independence; could delay confirmation into June and create window where market reprices the regime shift risk.
